city_metadata.city_summary:
Norfolk (locally   NOR-fək) is an independent city in the U.S. commonwealth of Virginia. It had a population of 238,005 at the 2020 census, making it the third-most populous city in Virginia and 100th-most populous city in the United States. The city holds a strategic position as the historical, urban, financial, and cultural center of the Hampton Roads metropolitan area (sometimes called "Tidewater"), which has more than 1.8 million inhabitants and is the 37th-largest metropolitan area in the U.S.
Norfolk was established in 1682 as a colonial seaport.

tags.name: USS Norfolk Bell
tags.note: The work is a cast brass bell in a steel yoke, with the text “USS Norfolk’ in raised letters on the bell surface. The work is sited on the waterfront with views to the Navy ships.

tags.name: Museum 4 Watton
tags.note: Free entry. Entrance is on the side of the building (Hall Alley). Museum is the front half of Wayland Hall.

tags.description: Privately owned military museum with rides on army vehicles, plus over 25 tanks and military vehicles, small arms and uniforms.
tags.name: The Muckleburgh Military Collection

tags.description: Traditional sheep breed farming and sheep dog training. Plans to plant a traditional orchard. Footpaths around north and south fields. Red squirrel breeding enclosures.
tags.name: Mayfields Farm
Gooderstone Water Gardens and Nature Trail
Anchor;USS Antietam Anchor
tags.note: The work is a surplus battleship anchor of unknown alloy. It sits directly on a paved asphalt walkway leading into Town Point Park. A small plaque identified the object as a naval relic.

tags.wheelchair:description: There is level access from the main entrance to: The Hall, Ticket Office, Stables Courtyard, Courtyard Café and Gift Shop, Lavatories, Holkham Stories Exhibition, Walled Garden.

Thurne Dyke Windpump
tags.note: A wind powered drainage pump, not a windmill, this drains water from the dykes pumping it into the river. It has never milled anything, though it is sometimes referred to as a mill (Morse's Mill)
